I agree with the selection of option A. The chamfer finish line enhances marginal integrity and aesthetic outcomes specifically on the lingual surface where direct contact with soft tissues occurs. This design provides better protection against microleakage by creating a more precise margin fit between the metal substrate and ceramics, especially important in areas like the lingual side of metal-ceramic crowns.
